title: Bhikkhus, there are these three things. What three? Greed, hatred, delusion. Bhikkhus, these are the three things. For the abandonment of those three things, bhikkhus,…
content: Bhikkhus, there are these three things. What three? Greed, hatred, delusion. Bhikkhus, these are the three things. For the abandonment of those three things, bhikkhus, three things should be developed. What three? For the abandonment of greed, the perception of foulness should be developed. For the abandonment of hatred, loving-kindness should be developed. For the abandonment of delusion, wisdom should be developed. For the abandonment of those three things, bhikkhus, these three things should be developed.

title: Bhikkhus, what is the course of practice that is pleasant with slow direct knowledge? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu, secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome…
content: Bhikkhus, what is the course of practice that is pleasant with slow direct knowledge? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu, secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states, enters and abides in the first jhāna ... with the stilling of initial and sustained thought, enters and abides in the second jhāna ... with the fading away of rapture, enters and abides in the third jhāna ... with the abandoning of pleasure and pain, enters and abides in the fourth jhāna ... He lives depending on the five learner's powers: the power of faith, the power of conscience, the power of fear of wrongdoing, the power of energy, the power of wisdom. For him, these five faculties appear very soft: the faculty of faith, the faculty of energy, the faculty of mindfulness, the faculty of concentration, the faculty of wisdom. Because these five faculties appear very soft, he attains the immediate concentration for the destruction of the taints slowly. This, bhikkhus, is called the course of practice that is pleasant with slow direct knowledge. And, bhikkhus, what is the course of practice that is pleasant with quick direct knowledge? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu, secluded from sensual pleasures ... enters and abides in the fourth jhāna ... He lives depending on the five learner's powers ... For him, these five faculties appear very keen: the faculty of faith ... the faculty of wisdom. Because these five faculties appear very keen, he attains the immediate concentration for the destruction of the taints quickly. This, bhikkhus, is called the course of practice that is pleasant with quick direct knowledge.

title: I ask the sage of great wisdom, who has gone beyond, at peace, self-established. How should a bhikkhu, leaving home and abandoning sensual pleasures, Properly…
content:
I ask the sage of great wisdom, who has gone beyond, at peace, self-established.
How should a bhikkhu, leaving home and abandoning sensual pleasures,
Properly wander in the world?

The Blessed One:
A bhikkhu who subdues greed for pleasures,
In the divine and human realms, beyond existence, knowing the Dhamma —
That bhikkhu properly wanders in the world.

A bhikkhu who has abandoned divisive speech, anger, and stinginess,
With all opposition completely destroyed —
That bhikkhu properly wanders in the world.

title: Standing to one side, the devata Sudatta spoke this verse before the Blessed One: Like a sword touching the skin, Like fire burning the head,…
content:
Standing to one side, the devata Sudatta spoke this verse before the Blessed One:
Like a sword touching the skin,
Like fire burning the head,
A bhikkhu should be mindful,
Gone forth, abandoning sensual desires.
( Blessed One ):
Like a sword touching the skin,
Like fire burning the head,
A bhikkhu should be mindful,
Gone forth, abandoning identity view.

title: Bhikkhus, there are these three kinds of good conduct. What three? Good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, and good mental conduct. Bhikkhus, these are the…
content:
Bhikkhus, there are these three kinds of good conduct. What three? Good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, and good mental conduct. Bhikkhus, these are the three kinds of good conduct.

Abandoning evil bodily action,
Abandoning evil verbal action,
Abandoning evil mental action,
And whatever else is blameworthy,

Not doing evil,
Doing much that is wholesome,
At the breaking up of the body,
One of superior wisdom
Is reborn in heaven.

title: And, bhikkhus, what is wrong view? There is no giving, no offering, no sacrifice; no fruit or result of good and bad actions; no this…
content: And, bhikkhus, what is wrong view? There is no giving, no offering, no sacrifice; no fruit or result of good and bad actions; no this world, no next world; no mother, no father; no spontaneously reborn beings; no samaṇas or brahmins in the world who, having practiced rightly, have realized and declared this world and the next by their own direct knowledge. Thus, bhikkhus, is wrong view.

title: - Bhikkhus, these ten things should be constantly reflected upon by one who has gone forth. What ten? One who has gone forth should constantly…
content: - Bhikkhus, these ten things should be constantly reflected upon by one who has gone forth. What ten? One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'I have now entered the state of being casteless.' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'My livelihood depends on others.' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'My deportment should be different (from laypeople).' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'Do I reproach myself regarding virtue?' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'Do my wise fellow celibates, after examination, reproach me regarding virtue?' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'All things that are dear and agreeable to me are subject to change and alteration.' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'I am the owner of my kamma, heir of my kamma, born of my kamma, related to my kamma, supported by my kamma; whatever kamma I will do, good or evil, of that I will be the heir.' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'How do I spend my days and nights? What has become of me?' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'Do I delight in empty huts or not?' One who has gone forth should constantly reflect: 'Have I attained a superior human state, a truly noble distinction of knowledge and vision, so that when questioned by my fellow celibates on my last days, I will not be ashamed?' Bhikkhus, these ten things should be constantly reflected upon by one who has gone forth.

title: Elder Heraññakāni: Day and night pass, life diminishes, Human life-span decays like water in a small stream. The fool doing evil deeds does not understand…
content:
Elder Heraññakāni:

Day and night pass, life diminishes,
Human life-span decays like water in a small stream.

The fool doing evil deeds does not understand his own action.
Afterwards he suffers when the evil ripens.

title: Elder Khaṅkhārevata: See this wisdom of the Tathāgatas, Like a fire burning at midnight, Giving light, giving sight, They subdue the doubts of those who…
content:
Elder Khaṅkhārevata:

See this wisdom of the Tathāgatas,
Like a fire burning at midnight,
Giving light, giving sight,
They subdue the doubts of those who come.

title: - A fellow bhikkhu, Venerable Sāriputta, has given up the training and returned to the lower life." - "Friend, that happens to one who does…
content: - A fellow bhikkhu, Venerable Sāriputta, has given up the training and returned to the lower life." - "Friend, that happens to one who does not guard the sense faculties, does not know moderation in eating, and is not devoted to wakefulness. 'Friend, a bhikkhu who does not guard the sense faculties, does not know moderation in eating, and is not devoted to wakefulness — for life, that one could live the holy life completely pure — that is impossible.' 'Friend, a bhikkhu who guards the sense faculties, knows moderation in eating, and is devoted to wakefulness — for life, that one could live the holy life completely pure — that is possible. Therefore, friend, you should train thus: "We will guard the sense faculties, know moderation in eating, and be devoted to wakefulness." Thus, friend, you should train.
